chief_complaint: A 35-year-old woman presents with sudden onset shortness of breath and chest pain.
history: The patient has a history of interrupted inferior vena cava with azygos continuation and polysplenia. She has been experiencing symptoms of a pulmonary embolism, including sudden onset shortness of breath and chest pain. She has no history of previous pulmonary embolisms or deep vein thrombosis. She is on no medications.
exam: Vital signs: blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, heart rate 110 beats per minute, respiratory rate 24 breaths per minute. Physical examination reveals bilateral lung crackles and a loud S1 heart sound.
labs: ECG: normal sinus rhythm with T-wave inversion in leads V1-V3. Blood tests: D-dimer 500 ng/mL, INR 1.2, platelet count 250,000/μL. Imaging: CT pulmonary angiogram shows a large pulmonary embolism in the right lung.
